reasoning: The 10-year TIPS yield (DFII10) roughly follows a random walk with no strong drift in the very short term, as indicated by a lag-1 autocorrelation near zero in daily data. For random walk variables, the probability of exceeding the current value is technically 50%, adjusted downward slightly for the probability of an exact tie (since the index provides values to two decimal places). With a daily standard deviation of ~0.045%, the chance of a tie decreases over time, making the true probability of an increase slowly converge toward 0.5. In the absence of strong structural evidence for a sudden spike or plunge, a flat prediction near 0.5 is optimal, with probabilities converging perfectly to 50% for the longer horizons.
